description: Portrait-orientation quote card. The border carries diagonal multicolored stripes (orange, teal, mustard, black, cream). At top, a circular photo medallion with a layered orange, teal, and black ring shows a smiling woman with long dark hair, orange paint markings on her cheeks, wearing a beaded white and green sash and holding a feather fan. The center is a cream panel with a large black quotation mark and dark serif body text reading: 'Native American Heritage Month is important to me because it's a month to celebrate my culture, to bring awareness to our people and our history. There are stories of my culture that others may not fully understand, but I can bring awareness and truth about my people and the challenges we faced, not just for this month, but every single day.' Attribution in bold: 'Jessica, Immigration Service Assistant, USCIS.' The circular DHS seal appears at bottom-right.
notable_text: 'Native American Heritage Month is important to me because it's a month to celebrate my culture, to bring awareness to our people and our history...' — Jessica, Immigration Service Assistant, USCIS
